item.title: Planet Amvlys
item.description:
Overview

Planet Amvlys was a student project I created for my game design course at the University of East London. I was the lead designer on the project within a team of 6 students. The project was developed within unreal engine 5 from September 2025 - May 2026.

My role for the project, was to write the story, concept all items, upgrades, and enemy’s, as well as designing the level and all of its unique player interactions such as puzzles and lore logs. throughout this project, I learned quite a lot about team management, as well as further developing my understanding of UE5

item.title: Mirari
item.description:
Overview

Mirari was made for the Ukie Student Game Jam 2026 that took place over 26-27th of February and lasted 32 hours.

The theme of the jam was symmetry, based on this we developed a Sokoban-style puzzle game where you play as a sentient mirror who instead of pushing boxes, reflect them to the other side of yourself. This leads to unique puzzle designs where positioning and distances are very important. There are also some additional mechanics you will discover as you play.

item.title: FPS demo
item.description:
Overview

This Is a very small project, I wanted to work on testing high maneuverability mechanics I had worked on previously, and pairing them with an fps style level. admittedly It didn’t end up too great, but I did end up learning quite a lot from it, which I am now applying to my current projects.

Gameplay

Shoot your way through the haunted house!

Collect Items!

Get Out!

item.title: First person frogger
item.description:
Overview

This is a very small game that I challenged myself to create in only 2 days. Its exactly what its says on the tin, It’s frogger but in first person. The objective is simple, navigate past deadly obstacles to make your way towards picking up The doughnut

Gameplay

Jump around!

Navigate deadly obstacles!

Get the doughnut!

Play Here

bakerboy16.itch.io

Credits

Unity Game Engine

item.title: Horror game demo
item.description:
Overview

A small horror game I made in a week, I wanted try my hand at creating an eerie environment, as well as enemy chase AI programming. It ended up being fun to learn and create, also learnt a lot about designing a maze in which you get chased around.

Gameplay

search for levers!

Avoid the eyes!

Get Out!

Link

Play Here

bakerboy16.itch.io

Credits

Unity Game Engine

item.title: Are you sure you want to play this?
item.description:
Overview

In this game demo you play as a beta tester, going through an assortment of different games and “play testing” them for a developer.

This was made for a Uni project with the theme given being “Comedy”. This project was developed using Unity Game Engine. I have no plans to continue development at the moment.

Gameplay

Play through an assortment of experiences

give feedback

ESCAPE

item.title: Mocap directing experience
item.description:
Overview

For a University project I was given the chance to write and direct a new mocap scene for a pre existing game as an imaginary DLC. this was all made possible via a collaboration between The university of east London and Target3D. The game I choose to write this imaginary DLC cutscene for was Metal Gear Solid 3. The scene I wrote, was about two of the games antagonists, Revolver Ocelot, and The Boss, getting a moment alone together and finally confronting each other about being mother and son, and why The Boss left her son.

item.title: Going Home
item.description:
Overview

Going Home is the second game I ever worked on. It was made for a Uni project. The objective of the game is to try to make your way home without suffering from an anxiety attack. The theme I was given for the project was to make a narrative emotional experience

Gameplay

Make your way home!

Avoid all social interaction!

Don’t get close!

Play Here

bakerboy16.itch.io
